result:
**Proposed goal: Add a CRISPR-Cas9 Gene Editing & Repair simulation mode.**

This would model the molecular mechanics of CRISPR gene editing on a 2D DNA strand visualization: guide RNA scanning along a double helix searching for PAM sequences, Cas9 binding and double-strand break (DSB) cutting, competing DNA repair pathways (NHEJ error-prone rejoining with indel mutations vs HDR template-directed precise repair), off-target cleavage at partially-matching sites, and — at the population level — gene drive propagation through a breeding population showing super-Mendelian inheritance spreading an edited allele. Presets could include Precise Gene Knockout, HDR Knock-In, Off-Target Mutagenesis, Gene Drive Spread, Base Editing (nCas9), and Prime Editing. This fills a gap between the existing DNA helix visualization and protein folding modes by simulating the most consequential biotech tool of the decade.
